column1: Anthem MDX Distribution Amplifier Serial Commands

column2: Description
(semicolon) Command separator/terminator - note that semicolon is the only valid line feed.
column3: there will be a semicolon at the end of a query

column2: Upper case letters represent command, lower case represent variables which must be entered.

"Zx" at the beginning of a command represents zone selection.
  MDX-16: x=1 to 8
  MDX-8: x = 1 to 4

Set zone x power. 
x=zone: 0 (all), 1=zone 1 ../. 8=zone 8 
to y: 
  0=off
  1=on
  t=toggle. 
When unit is in 1/2W standby mode, it may be necessary to send the power-on command twice. Ex:"Z0POW1;Z0POW1;". The first instance is the wake-up from low-power state. Note that Zx=Z0 applies only to power command.
column3:
ZxPOW? returns ZxPOWy

Z0POW? returns ZxPOWy for all zones (Z1POWy;Z2POWy;Z3PO…;Z8POWy;)

column2:
Query if ARC (Anthem Room Correction) is valid for zone x
  0=invalid
  1=valid.

Turn ARC (Anthem Room Correction) in zone x to y:
  0=off
  1=on
Returns an error if an attempt to turn it on is made when there are no ARC co-efficients available.

Set zone name: yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy=name. 
Maximum length of the zone name is 16 ASCII characters. 
Note that the character ";" is prohibited as it is used as a terminator.

Input Select: nn=input number. 
  01=Analog 1, 02=Analog 2, ... 08=Analog 8
  09=Digital 1 ... 12=Digital 4.
MDX-8 supports only input 01, 02, 03, 04 and 09.

Set zone x listening mode 
  y=s for stereo
  y=m for mono (l+r)
  y=l for left only
  y= r for right only
Must be set to stereo when stereo bridge mode is required.
Must be set to mono when mono bridge mode is required.

Set speaker profile of zone x to brand bb, line ll, model mm. 
Numbers start at 01.
column3: ZxSPK? returns ZxSPKbbllmm. If there is no speaker assigned ( i.e. generic), it will return ZxSPK010101

column2:
Speaker profile enable
  y=0 don't use speaker model profile
  y=1 apply current speaker model profile

Volume setting: yyy.y=value (0.0 to 100.0). If setting integer values, it is not necessary to include the decimal. 
Example: Z1VOL35 represents zone 1 volume set to 35.  Entry is rounded to nearest valid value.

Volume up: yy.y=dB step: 0.0 to 10.0, m=minimum (0.5 dB). 
Entry is rounded to nearest valid value

Volume down: yy.y=dB step: 0.0 to 10.0, m= minimum (0.5 dB). 
Entry is rounded to nearest valid value.

Mute: 
  0=unmute
  1=mute
  t=toggle
Power On volume setting: yyy.y=value (0.0 to 100.0).  Example: Z1POV35 represents zone 1 power-up volume set to 35.  Entry is rounded to nearest valid value.
Maximum volume setting: yyy.y=value (0.0 to 100.0).  Example: Z1MXV75 represents zone 1 max volume set to 75.  Entry is rounded to nearest valid value.
Zone level offset. yyy.y=value (-12.0 to +6.0 [dB] in 0.5 dB steps, default 0.0)

Tone setting: 
  y=0 bass
  y=1 treble  
  s=sign: +/- 
  zz.z=value  
Range: -10.0 to +10.0 (step size of 0.5 dB)
Example: Z1TON0-01 represents a bass cut by 1 dB.

Tone up: 
  y=0 bass
  y=1 treble
  zz.z=dB step
Range: 0.5 to 10.0 (step size of 0.5 dB)

Tone down: 
  y=0 bass
  y=1 treble
 zz.z=dB step
Range: 0.5 to 10.0 (step size of 0.5 dB)

Set the speaker channel 'c' (Table 2) to have a level offset 'syy.y'. s=sign: +/-, yy.y= dB value. 
Range -12.0 to 12.0

Query audio input format: 
  0=no input (if digital)
  1=Analog
  2= PCM
  10= unsupported
Unsupported format will be reported if a Dolby or DTS stream is detected.
Query audio input sampling rate (kHz)

For the specified zone x, set the sub mode y to:
  0=disabled
  1=enabled
  t=toggle

For the specified zone x, set the crossover 'yyy' (0=Off/Bypass (fullrange speaker/Sub), 40=40Hz, 50=50Hz, 60=60Hz, etc..)
yyy should be between 20 and 180 Hz (in 1 Hz increment)
For the specified zone x, set sub phase to 'ppp' (0 - 180) degrees

For the specified zone x, set the sub polarity to 'p' 
  0=normal
  1=inverted
0=not bridged, 1=bridged. Set listening mode to stereo or mono depending on desired mode of operation.
Works only on odd numbered zones. 0 = normal, 1 = Use the bridged output of the next zone as the sub output.
Input numbers start at 1

Set trim level of specified Input.
  nn=input number
  s=sign: +/-
  yy.y=value
Example: IPT08-3.5 represents analog input 8 trim set to -3.5 dB.  Entry is rounded to nearest valid value. Range -20.0 to 20.0 dB

Set input name nn=input number, yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy=name. Maximum length of the input name is 16 ASCII characters. Eg. ISN02Analog1. Note that the character ";" is prohibited as it is used as a terminator.

Set Auto Off Delay: 
  0=disabled (no auto off) 
  1=5 minutes
  2=10 minutes
  3=20 minutes
  4=1 hour
  5=2 hours
  6=6 hours
Set Auto On Delay: 0-20 seconds

Set front panel brightness: 
  0=off
  1=dim
  2=high
  n=cycle to next
Query model name
column3: returns IDM followed by model e.g. "IDMMDX-8"

column2:
Device name: yyyyyyyyyyyyyyyy is a name of up to 16 characters. 
Semi-colon is NOT a valid character (as it is used as a terminator).
Query firmware version
column3: returns IDS followed by software version e.g. "IDS0.1.4931"

column2: Get the serial number of the unit.
column3: returns GSN followed by the serial number (up to 15 characters)

column2: Query model and firmware version
column3: returns IDQ followed by model, software version, region, software build date e.g. "IDQMDX16 0.1.4931 12/19/18 08:49"

column2: Query bootloader version, build date, build time
column3: returns IBL followed by the information. E.g.IBLv0.2.8491 2019/03/13 12:00:05;

column2: Query software build date
column3: returns IDB followed by software build date e.g. "IDB12/19/16"

column2: Query hardware version
column3: returns IDH followed by hardware version e.g. "IDH3"

column2: Query operational mode set by the ON MODE switch at the back of the unit.
column3:
returns MOD followed by:
  0 for Audio Detect
  1 for Trigger
  2 for Ext. Command.

column2: Query number of speaker brands
column3: returns SPB followed by the number of speaker brands.

column2: Query name of brand number bb.
column3: returns SPBbb followed by name of brand

column2: Query number of speaker lines in brand bb
column3: returns SPLbb followed by the number of speaker lines in the brand.

column2: Query name of line number ll in brand number bb.
column3: returns SPLbbll followed by name of the line

column2: Query number of speaker models in line ll in brand bb
column3: returns SPMbbll followed by the number of speaker models in that line in the brand.

column2: Query name of model number mm in line ll in brand number bb.
column3: returns SPMbbllmm followed by name of the model

column2:
Set IP mode to:
  0=DHCP
  1=manual
IP Address This is only valid if IP mode is manual
Subnet Mask. This is only valid if IP mode is manual.
IP Gateway This is only valid if IP mode is manual
Query MAC Address
column3: returns MAC followed by MAC address e.g. "MAC7CB77B000353;"

column2:
Load current settings with:
  u=user settings
  i=installer settings
  f=factory defaults
column3:
LSTy? Returns LSTya where 
  a=0 means there are none saved 
  a=1 means that settings are available

column2:
Save current settings as
  u=user settings
  i=installer settings
  c=current (The MDX periodically checks current settings to see if any settings have changed and does a save if they have. The SSTc command forces a save.)

Global Mute where y 
  0=unmute
  1=mute
  t=toggle

Set the digital output source of the MDX-16 to n: 1=Digital 1, 2=Digital 2, 
3=Digital 3, 4=Digital 4

Unit ID 
  0=off
  1=rear LED on
  2=rear LED flash
  3=rear and power LEDs flash
Query fan speed.
column3: returns FAN followed by the curent fan speed in RPM

column2: Set fan speed 0-100
Query power supply Voltage.
Query power supply temperature.
column3: returns PST followed by the temperature in Celcius

column2: Get AMP Temperature for specified zone.
Query Status
column3: Returns ZxSTATyy with hexadecimal bitmask yy which is the bitwise OR of each error as specified in the 'Error Bits Definition' - table 3 below. Ex: 10 would be short detected.

column2: Query error count: y selects the count to query - see Table 4: 'Error Count definition'.
column3: Returns ZxECNTyn where n is the error count.

column2: Clear the error counts.

column1: 1. Successful serial commands return ';' after the action is completed.

2. When a recognized command cannot be executed "!E<OriginalCommand>" is returned. Ex: Analog 5 is selected on the 4-zone amp (which does not exist) by sending the command "Z1INP5;", then the system would return "!EZ1INP5;".
3. Out-of-range parameters return the message "!E<OriginalCommand>". Ex: 'Z1VOL101;' would result in a response: '!EZ1VOL101;'
4. Invalid commands return the message "!I<OriginalCommand>". Ex 'HELLO;' would result in a response '!IHELLO;'
5. Receiving a command for a zone when the zone is off (but the system is not in standby), returns the message "!Z<OriginalMessage>".
6. Ony a few commands are valid when the system is in standby such as 'IDM?', 'ZxPOWy', 'ZxPOW?'. All other commands are considered 'Invalid'.
7. Maximum command length is 256 bytes including the terminator. Maximum response length is 258 bytes.
8. Average command processing latency is less than 30 ms.
9. Individual commands have a maximum processing latency of 100 ms.
10. Systems that communicate over RS232 should wait for at least 1 s for a response before retransmission

11. Control system should behave as a "good citizen" and try to minimize the amount of redundant control commands. For example, in the case of a volume slider, not every intermediate/temporary volume settings need to be sent (Z1VOL52;Z1VOL54;Z1VOL56; is preferable and is equivalent to Z1VOL50;Z1VOL51;Z1VOL52;Z1VOL53;Z1VOL54;Z1VOL55;Z1VOL56;)
12. A websocket connection can be established using the following javascript snippet: var socket = new WebSocket('ws://' + ip + '/cmd');
13. Optionnally you can discover supported devices using the discovery protocol outline in the Discovery Protocol tab.
14. A TCP/IP connection to the unit must be established on port 14999.
15. We are regularly testing 4 simultaneous "good citizen" control connections.
